key_insight: AdvancedMathBench shows that frontier LLMs still struggle with advanced mathematical proof generation and, especially, reliable proof verification.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark and automated verification pipeline provide a stronger evaluation framework for testing and improving reasoning systems on rigorous math tasks beyond final-answer scoring.
method: ProverBench: 296 advanced proof problems spanning undergraduate and doctoral qualifying-exam levels.
Automatic proof-verification pipeline trained on large-scale expert annotations to score correctness and diagnose proof errors.
VerifierBench: 888 model-generated proof trajectories with expert ground truth for judging validity and verification rationales.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong agreement with human experts for the verifier and performance ceilings that remain modest: GPT-5.5-xhigh reaches only 75.8 and 66.1 on proof generation, while the best verification model achieves Balanced F1 of 65.1.
watch_item: Because evaluation depends on an automated verifier trained from expert annotations, deployment should watch for domain shift and error modes that may be underrepresented in the training data.
paper_title: AdvancedMathBench: A Benchmark Suite for Advanced Mathematical Proof Generation and Verification
